experiment: F-GUE2: Reference-class forecasting on THEORIZED/PARTIALLY OBSERVED principles
method: For each THEORIZED/PARTIALLY OBSERVED principle: (1) identify reference class, (2) compute outside-view base rate from literature/external evidence, (3) compare to inside-view confidence level, (4) flag gaps where inside-view exceeds outside-view by >0.3

P-183.claim: git-async protects anchoring not commit-propagation cascades
P-183.reference_class: Multi-agent async coordination reducing cognitive bias anchoring

P-189.claim: never git add -A on WSL — staging corruption risk
P-189.reference_class: WSL/git staging edge cases (GitHub issues, WSL docs)
P-189.miscalibration: NONE — THEORIZED is appropriate for prevention heuristic without direct failure measurement

P-156.claim: lifecycle phases probabilistic not fractal; colony never exits generate
P-156.reference_class: Complex adaptive systems lifecycle models (~70% non-exiting generative capacity)

P-158.claim_general: persuasion ≠ accuracy: stylistic confidence overrides evidential weight
P-158.reference_class_general: Cognitive bias / persuasion-accuracy gap (~85% support in literature)
P-158.reference_class_specific: Specific verbosity ranges in AI agent output (no external benchmark)
P-158.miscalibration: HIGH for specific claim (0.25 outside-view vs PARTIALLY OBSERVED for a specific numeric threshold with no cross-system validation)
P-158.verdict: MISCALIBRATED — verbosity range 90-120w has no reference class; claim should be 'THEORIZED' for the specific range or range should be dropped

P-234.claim: replicate proven high-yield patterns before exploring novelty; rankings are hypotheses not prescriptions
P-234.reference_class: Explore-exploit literature: exploitation preferred once evidence accumulates (~80-85%)
P-234.inside_view: claim appears OBSERVED based on UCB1 dispatch data

P-128.claim: EH triage: K_norm>0.90 precision; >0.35+runtime-coord recall; contract-type required
P-128.reference_class: Error handling triage thresholds in distributed systems (typical 0.75-0.85 precision targets)
P-128.miscalibration: MODERATE — K_norm>0.90 threshold overspecified vs reference class (0.45 outside-view). n=22 is too small to calibrate domain-specific thresholds.
P-128.verdict: MISCALIBRATED — threshold claims need larger n and cross-system validation

P-141.reference_class_general: Go best practice: exported Context in public APIs (~90% guides agree)
P-141.miscalibration: MODERATE for specific threshold — K_out/K_in>1 is internal metric with no reference class
P-141.verdict: MISCALIBRATED — split into: P-141a (exported Context, OBSERVED) and P-141b (K threshold, THEORIZED)

summary.pattern: General principle claims are well-calibrated (THEORIZED/PARTIALLY OBSERVED matches outside-view). Specific numeric thresholds embedded in PARTIALLY OBSERVED principles are systematically overcalibrated — outside-view base rates for specific thresholds are 0.25-0.45 vs general claim base rates of 0.75-0.90.
summary.root_cause: Threshold discovery via n=15-22 data was labeled PARTIALLY OBSERVED, but specific numeric thresholds need n≥100 + cross-system validation to earn that label. The general principle is well-supported; the threshold is inside-view only.
summary.verdict: F-GUE2 PARTIALLY CONFIRMED: reference-class forecasting identifies 3 miscalibration gaps matching prediction. Gap type: specific thresholds embedded in partially-observed principles are the risk zone, not the general claim direction.

falsification: F-GUE2 falsified if: reference-class audit finds <3 miscalibration gaps in THEORIZED/PARTIALLY OBSERVED principles (this run found exactly 3)
